csharp.note: 1 failure is pre-existing LLM flake (Query_ToolCall_LlmRequestsToolAndHandlesError) unchanged by this work

plang.note: All .test.goal files renamed correctly but none has a .pr on disk — discovery labels them Stale. LLM builder must run to materialize .pr files before these can execute.

findings.issue: 16 of 19 .test.goal files still contain 'throw "not implemented"' as the only step. Rename from .goal → .test.goal is done, but no .pr files exist. Discovery labels all 19 as Stale. Once built, the 16 stubs will Fail (not Skip) because their body is the throw.
findings.impact: The PLang end-to-end pipeline (LLM builder → .pr → GoalMapper → runtime) has zero green integration tests today. A regression in any of those layers would not surface in CI until a human runs plang --test after a build.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) run `plang p build` locally (with LLM access) and commit the .pr files for the 3 tests with real bodies, OR (b) use @skip tags on the 16 stubs so they don't fail the suite once built.

findings.issue: Production coverage subscriber at run.cs:96-116 (the block that reads result.Properties["branchLabel"] / ["branchChain"] and calls Coverage.RecordBranchLabel/Chain) remains 0% covered. OrchestrateBranchCoverageTests replicates the filter shape via a hand-written subscriber on _app.User.Context.Events but never routes through test.run.
findings.impact: A typo in the production Properties keys (e.g. "branchIndx" instead of "branchIndex") or a wrong cast (as List<int> instead of as List<string>) would ship with 2268 tests passing.
findings.suggestion: Add a RunActionTests fixture whose goal contains a condition.if so test.run's real subscriber fires. After the run, assert _app.Testing.Coverage.BranchLabels[<site>] is populated from the production path.

findings.issue: MultiActionOrchestrate_InnerElseIfMatches... asserts `subran == 1` with a comment stating 'DisableChildrenOf worked'. But in the bug case (symptom 3 of the three-bug cluster), DisableChildrenOf is silently skipped — so the sub-step still runs because nothing disabled it. `subran == 1` is consistent with both bug AND fix; the assertion doesn't discriminate.
findings.impact: Weakens the reasoning trail of an otherwise-strong guard. Other assertions in the same test (Branches.ContainsKey('?:?') == false, IsFirstConditionInStep==false for inner) still discriminate, so the test overall is a real guard.
findings.suggestion: Either remove that assertion, or add a second sub-step under only the false branch (e.g. 'subran_false') and assert that variable is unset — actually testing disable-per-branch.

findings.issue: Executor.Run(args) (lines 17-22) is 0% covered — all ExecutorTests exercise Configure(args), not Run. Run = Configure + Start. The composition (propagating a Configure error without calling Start, vs invoking Start on success) is untested.
findings.impact: A regression where Run ignores the Configure error and calls Start anyway would not be caught.
findings.suggestion: Add `Run_InvalidConfig_ReturnsErrorWithoutStarting` — one small test. Pass `--test={"timeout":-1}`, assert Run returns the error data without Start side-effects.

findings.issue: RunSingleAsync catches at lines 139 (OperationCanceledException) and 143 (catch-all except OOM/SO) are 0% covered. Timeout test reaches Complete(Timeout) via the cts.IsCancellationRequested branch at line 134, not via the OCE catch. No fixture throws unexpectedly.
findings.impact: Low — these are safety nets. The docstring's 'never throws for child-test failures' claim is formally unproven.
findings.suggestion: Optional: a fixture handler that throws InvalidOperationException. Low priority.

deletion_test_results.note: For each tautology-fix rewritten in v2, I applied the deletion test: would the test still pass if I deleted the core assertion block?

tests_checked.assertion: Variables contains 'score' AND value round-trips as 42L
parses MyGoal:3: line specifically, asserts 0 and 1 and { and } in that line

concerns_checked: subscriber leak — all RunActionTests use try/finally to -= the handler
parallel-test contamination — each probe filters by childApp.AbsolutePath.StartsWith(_tempDir)
production surface — internal static; only PLang.Tests sees it via InternalsVisibleTo
emission order — invoked AFTER coverageBinding registration, BEFORE RunGoalAsync; probes see child state as the test will
childappcreated_hook_audit.verdict: accepted — test-only hook is narrow and correctly guarded

summary: Coder v2 addressed 15 of 17 v3 findings fully. The two partially-addressed findings are both PLang .test.goal pipeline items blocked on LLM builder access. Five minor residuals remain (F1-F5). All tautology fixes pass the deletion test — they genuinely probe child-App state rather than faking it. Suite 2267/2268 pass (same pre-existing flake). Coverage deltas on touched files are strong. Recommend security review next.
